llm_extracted.ai_arm_selection_rationale: Only one AI arm available (human_ai). Comparing human-AI collaboration (n=1258) vs human-human collaboration (n=976).
llm_extracted.notes: After re-reading the full PDF, the paper still does not report means, SDs, SEs, t-statistics, F-statistics, or standardized effect sizes (Cohen's d or Hedges' g) for the direct comparison between human_ai and human_human teams on text quality. Table 2 (page 22) shows only the regression coefficient β = 0.327 (SE 0.024) with p < 0.001 (inequality only). Table G (page 51) provides the full regression model with covariates but still only reports coefficients, not raw means or SDs. The paper uses regression-based analysis throughout rather than reporting descriptive statistics by arm. Without means+SDs, t-statistics, F-statistics, or reported effect sizes, Hedges' g cannot be computed. The p-value is reported only as an inequality (p < 0.001), not an exact value. Sample sizes are confirmed from Table 1 (page 16): n_human=976, n_ai=1258.
source_quotes: Table 2: Collaborating with AI agents improves productivity and text quality but lowers image quality (i.e., a jagged frontier), and decreases output diversity. Human-AI: β = 0.327*** (SE 0.024) for Text quality
Notes: *p<0.05, **p<0.01, ***p<0.001. Productivity is the number of submissions by participants. Text, Image, Click are human ratings (7-point scale) with standard errors clustered at the ad level.
